codeReview.summary: The feature mostly aligns with VAL-INDEX-009..013 and adds meaningful reliability tests plus rebuild/re-embed pagination plumbing. However, the new offset pagination queries do not use a total deterministic ordering key, which can skip or duplicate rows across pages under tie conditions and undermines the full-corpus guarantee.

issues.description: Offset pagination orders only by COALESCE(endTime,startTime,indexedAt), which is not a unique total order. Rows with equal timestamps can shift ordering between page reads, causing missed or duplicated conversations during rebuild pagination. Add a deterministic tie-breaker (e.g., id) to guarantee full-corpus coverage. Same pattern appears in other paginated queries used by this feature (SearchIndexStore.swift:81 and ArtifactStore.swift:227).

The new pagination tests do not cross the configured page boundary (rebuild/re-embed page size is 1000, but tests use 25 and 15 conversations), so they validate enqueue/re-embed behavior but not multi-page traversal correctness. Add fixtures that exceed a page to prevent false confidence for VAL-INDEX-011.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure requires reading .factory/library/user-testing.md, but this worker transcript skeleton does not show that step even though handoff.skillFeedback reports followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file: .factory/skills/indexing-efficiency-worker/SKILL.md step 1 lists user-testing.md; transcript skeleton for worker aa59acb8-a717-48bd-a959-620ff88463e7 includes reads for mission/AGENTS/architecture/services but no user-testing.md reference.

summary: Reviewed feature m3-indexing-reliability-recovery-and-requeue via handoff, transcript skeleton, skill contract, and commit 66924a8 diff. Found one blocking pagination-ordering correctness issue and one non-blocking test-coverage gap; marked feature review as fail pending deterministic pagination ordering fix.
